checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All headings and body text provided in the JSON can be directly traced back to the source PDF. The title and image alt text are acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility.

I have verified every phone number, email address, and URL present in the output against the source document. The email 'firsttech@nc.gov' and the URL for the FCC broadband speed guide are correct. While some contact information from omitted pages is missing, no information has been fabricated.

The output is missing a massive portion of the source document. Specifically, content from pages 5 through 23 and page 25 is entirely absent, as indicated by a placeholder section in the JSON stating '20 sections omitted for token budget'. This includes crucial maps, common barriers to adoption, executive order details, technical assistance team information, and details on the homework gap.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains 27 pages of content, including maps (pp. 5, 11), barriers to adoption (pp. 10, 12), and program details (pp. 17-23, 25).

checks.a_analysis: The tables present in the output (pages 3, 4, and 24) were compared row-for-row with the source. The 'Broadband Speeds' table (p. 3) contains all 19 rows. The 'Typical Broadband Usage' table (p. 4) contains all data rows. The 'GREAT Grant Program' table (p. 24) contains all 19 listed grant awardees.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are significant text omissions within existing sections. On page 2 ('Broadband Technology Types'), all descriptive sub-bullets for the technology types (DSL, Cable, Fiber, etc.) are missing, resulting in a loss of over 50% of the text for that page. Additionally, on page 4, the source URL following 'Source: ' is completely missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 contains detailed descriptions under technology types (e.g., DSL sub-bullet: 'Speeds range from 1.5 Mbps to 80 Mbps'). Page 4 contains the source link: 'https://www.fcc.gov/research-reports/guides/household-broadband-guide'.
checks.b_evidence_output: In section 2, the 'nested_list' for all technology types is null. In section 5 (page 4), the paragraph content for the source is just 'Source: '.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text present in the output accurately reflects the source document's meaning. Minor differences like the addition of a space in '12- 25 Mbps' or normalized heading levels for table accessibility are within the scope of acceptable document conversion changes.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks. While logos like 'NC DIT' appear repeatedly, this reflects their presence on every slide in the source document. No unique content is repeated more than in the original.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. Main slide titles are assigned H1 (e.g., 'What is Broadband?', 'Broadband Speeds'). Sub-headers like 'GREAT Awards' on Page 24 are correctly nested as H2 under 'GREAT Grant Program'.

The tables on pages 3, 4, and 24 are well-structured in the JSON. The 'Broadband Speeds' table on page 3 correctly handles category headers (e.g., 'Watching Video') as single-cell rows, and the multi-column data on page 4 is preserved with correct cell alignment. The two-column list on page 24 is also logically converted into a table format.
The reading order follows the logical flow of the presentation slides, progressing from titles to descriptions, lists, tables, and finally source links or logos.

The list on Page 2 ('Broadband Technology Types') is converted incorrectly. The source contains a nested structure where each main bullet has several sub-bullets (e.g., under 'Digital Subscriber Line', it lists 'Runs over traditional...' and 'Speeds range from...'). The JSON output flattens this list, including only the top-level categories and entirely omitting the sub-bulleted information from the list structure.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)
- Runs over traditional copper phone lines, speed dependent technology and distance
- Speeds range from 1.5 Mbps to 80 Mbps

checks.a_analysis: The URLs and contact emails present in the source (e.g., fcc.gov links on pages 3 and 4, and the email address on page 26) are preserved in the text content without errors or fabrications.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML follows a logical single-column vertical flow that accurately reflects the sequence of information in the original slide deck. While the layout transforms from landscape slides to a responsive vertical list, the organization of the text content remains consistent with the source's intent. Elements such as headings, lists, and tables appear in the same relative order as the source pages.

All tables in the document, specifically those on pages 3 (Broadband Speeds), 4 (Typical Broadband Usage), and 24 (GREAT Awards), are rendered with clear tabular structures. Headers are distinct, and the relationship between rows and columns is preserved, making the data easy to read and understand.

The HTML output fails to render nearly all of the meaningful imagery from the source PDF. This includes critical data maps (pages 5, 11, and 19), informational diagrams and infographics (pages 10, 12, 15, and 23), and data visualization grids (pages 6-9). In many cases, the HTML displays descriptive placeholders or alt-text (e.g., '[Two blue hexagon shapes...]', 'A pattern of home icons.', '[Abstract colorful wavy background...]') instead of the images themselves. This represents a significant loss of content that is central to the presentation's purpose.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains complex maps on pages 5, 11, and 19; data visualization grids on pages 6-9; and informational diagrams on pages 10, 12, 15, and 23.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows text placeholders such as 'A pattern of home icons.', 'Two blue hexagon shapes arranged horizontally...', and 'Abstract colorful wavy background with text overlay' where the meaningful content images should be.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections corresponding to PDF pages 5-12, 15, 19, 21-23, and 27.

checks.a_analysis: The document does not exhibit any visual duplication of body content. While headings like 'Executive Order 91' appear more than once, this correctly reflects the structure of the source document which contains multiple consecutive slides with the same title. The text and table content themselves are unique and follow the source's sequence without repetition.
